title: Cirrus: Add [CI:BUILD] magic that only builds
body:
This is intended for cases where no code changes were made in this repo.
but something did change which could affect the toolchain.  For example,
`Makefile` or packaging changes.

comments.body: This stuff ***really*** hurts my brain, but...I think I got it right?
Do we wanna merge this first and then get the podman.spec.rpkg change going as a test?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *lsm5* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Beats me; this long ago exceeded my limited grasp of yaml. But if it helps, here is (I think) the new task map:
![cirrus-map-pr13106](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/152045094-d236799d-e7e6-49dc-8539-f211d3729cdc.png)

> Beats me; this long ago exceeded my limited grasp of yaml. But if it helps, here is (I think) the new task map:

Oh, the YAML is correct, I just question the confusingly complex negative + positive logic involved :laughing: 

At one point I think you tried adding 'only' into the map-maker, and it broke your brain (and maybe the script).

In any case, the intent here is:

* For branches, only do some things (skipping all others)
* For tags, only do some different things (skipping all others)
* For [CI:DOCS] only do validation, some build, and swagger
* For [CI:BUILD] only do validation, build, OSX, and other Alt. build checks (like "test RPM")
Clarification: "YAML is correct" - it's valid YAML...it may not actually spell out my intentions.
Oof, I think the "upgrade test" logic is wrong.  @edsantiago didn't we decide a while ago, that test should _only_ run as part of the daily cron-jobs?
Okay, I think this is right now.
